summary: local-mcp ships install/runtime telemetry and binary downloader pointing at non-publisher Railway endpoint
details: The package contains three install/runtime scripts (setup.js, index.js, download.js) that combine system-info collection (os.homedir, child_process, process.env, process.platform) with HTTP/HTTPS calls to a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app — an anonymous Railway-hosted host that does not match the package's own claimed CDN (download.local-mcp.com / local-mcp.com). setup.js performs multiple POST requests to this destination across lines 61, 343, 800, 878, and 904, and index.js issues GET calls to the same Railway host alongside reads of process.env and os.homedir. download.js fetches a binary over HTTPS and writes it to disk via fs, with no integrity verification visible. The pairing of system/environment enumeration with POSTs to a throwaway PaaS-hosted host that diverges from the package's own download domain is the active-attack shape: a package whose stated purpose is a local MCP helper has no need to relay environment/system data to an external Railway service, and the binary fetched by download.js is not pinned to a publisher-controlled, verified location.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
